news.title: Chelsea’s good World Cup omens | News | Official Site
news.lede: The end of a World Cup is usually a good sign for Chelsea, with some of the most successful and important seasons in our history coming immediately after the international showpiece. The latest edition of the FIFA World Cup came to a close on Sunday evening, when Spain were crowned champions for the second time with a 1-0 win over Argentina in the 2026 final in New Jersey. People sometimes talk about a World Cup hangover, with some of the players who star at the tournament having difficult seasons afterwards as their summer exertions and late return for pre-season catch up with them. However, if history is anything to go by, that is unlikely to be the case for the 11 Chelsea players who competed at the 2026 World Cup, with the post-World Cup seasons producing some of the Blues’ most memorable campaigns.

news.title: The US should never host another tournament after World Cup debacle
news.lede: The moral, financial and logistical issues that plagued this World Cup should be enough to disqualify the US from hosting major football tournaments until serious changes are made, argues Richard Jolly. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: Argentina dreams of another Messi for the 2030 World Cup: the fact about his son Thiago that has attracted attention
news.lede: In the world of football, Leo Messi's immediate future is generating countless questions. However, among the sea of Argentine fans, a mathematical detail that until recently went unnoticed has gained traction, fueling the fans' greatest hopes. Popular attention has focused on the year 2030, the centenary of the World Cup. By then, Thiago Messi, the eldest son of the Rosario-born star, will have reached the age of majority, turning 18 in November.

news.title: Gaza celebrates World Cup victory of Spanish team they took into their hearts
news.lede: People gather at cafes and outdoor screenings to support team after Spain’s backing for Palestinian rights. The Spanish football team’s World Cup win has been celebrated in the streets of Gaza, where Spain’s support for Palestinian rights and Israel’s backing for the Argentinian team turned the competition into more than a football showdown. Gathered at cafes and outdoor screenings, Palestinians defied the risk of Israeli attacks to cheer on a team they had taken to their hearts. Many waving Spanish flags paid tribute to teenage star Lamine Yamal, who brought a Palestinian flag to celebrations for Barcelona’s La Liga win in May.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina react to final defeat and Messi questions
news.lede: Enzo Fernandez played in seven of Argentina's eight 2026 World Cup matches. Midfielder Enzo Fernandez has claimed Argentina represented their nation "in the best possible way" during the World Cup, despite ugly scenes during and after their final loss to Spain. Fernandez was sent off late in normal time for two yellow cards and Spain won 1-0 after extra time on Sunday in New Jersey. His first booking was for showing dissent towards Slovenian referee Slavko Vincic and Fernandez was later dismissed for catching Pau Cubarsi with a challenge that sent the defender cartwheeling in the air.

news.title: La Liga chief claims Fifa is ‘destroying football industry’ and calls for Infantino to quit
news.lede: Javier Tebas: ‘Fifa are making irresponsible decisions’. ‘Hydration breaks are a lie … it was a break for advertising’. La Liga’s president, Javier Tebas, has accused Fifa of “destroying the football industry” and called for its president, Gianni Infantino, to quit. Tebas spoke out as the dust settled on the 2026 World Cup with suggestions that the 2030 tournament could be increased from 48 to 64 teams.

news.title: David Squires on … Spain winning the World Cup final in the shadow of Trump
news.lede: Our cartoonist looks back at the final game of the 2026 World Cup as Donald Trump watched Spain triumph again. Buy this cartoon | Some of David’s favourite works. And his latest book, Chaos in the Box: get it now.

news.title: Argentina National Football Team Under Fire as FIFA Launches Investigation After World Cup Final Chaos
news.lede: Spain's 1-0 extra-time victory over the Argentina National Football Team in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final has taken another dramatic turn after FIFA opened a disciplinary investigation into post-match clashes involving several Argentina players. The incidents happened during Spain's trophy celebrations at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, one day after Spain lifted their first World Cup title since 2010. Videos from the pitch showed physical confrontations involving members of the Argentina National Football Team after the final whistle. Argentina finished the match with 10 players after Enzo Fernández received a second yellow card in stoppage time at the end of the 90 minutes.
